recalls.Description: GLMZZ is recalling its Fidget Magnet Ball Toy Sets due to safety concerns. Each set includes eight magnet balls in various colors—white, blue, green, pink, yellow, cream, and two purple balls—each measuring approximately 1.24 inches in diameter. The toys are packaged in a black zippered case, which may pose a risk to children if ingested. The high-powered magnets can attract one another or other metal items, potentially leading to severe health issues if swallowed.
recalls.Title: GLMZZ Fidget Magnet Ball Toy Sets Recalled Due to Serious Injury Risk from Magnet Ingestion
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled toys immediately, keep them away from children, and contact GLMZZ at GLMZZrecall@outlook.com for a full refund. They will need to dispose of the toys and send a photo of the disposal to the same email address.

recalls.Description: Stoney Games has recalled the Kluster Fun Tabletop Magnet Chess Games due to safety concerns involving loose magnets that pose a serious ingestion hazard. They are packaged in a black box displaying the name 'Kluster' and include around 24 magnets, an orange string, an instruction manual, and a black storage pouch also marked with 'Kluster.' Additionally, some games were sold in a white pouch with gameplay instructions on the back.

recalls.Description:
Joyreal busy board toys have been recalled due to serious safety concerns, including risks of choking and laceration. These toys feature a wooden base measuring approximately 6.6 inches by 6 inches and include various components such as 18 LED lights, a power switch, a brightness knob, a mirror, and more. The Joyreal logo and the word 'POWER' are visible on the front, with 'SKU: MX049-3' and 'Series no.20250730' marked on the underside.

The recall is prompted by the potential for the small mirror to detach, creating a choking hazard, and the presence of sharp edges on the mirror that pose a laceration risk. Parents are urged to take immediate action to ensure the safety of their children.
recalls.Title: Recall of Joyreal Busy Board Toys Due to Choking and Laceration Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Indream Store via email at indreamrecall@outlook.com.

The toys pose a serious choking hazard for children under three years old, as the eggs can obstruct a child's airway, leading to potential injury or death.

recalls.Description:
Gagaku-branded ice cream shop toy playsets are being recalled due to a serious safety hazard. These sets, which include 16 wooden pieces such as a cash register, ice cream cones, scoops, popsicles, and cupcakes, contain high-powered magnets that can detach and pose an ingestion risk to children. If swallowed, these magnets can attract each other or metal objects, leading to severe health issues including intestinal blockages and potential death.

Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled toys immediately and keep them away from children. The product features a pink display box labeled "ICE-CREAM" and has a label on the underside indicating "GAGAKU" and the model number KABI-0176 G062401IC. recalls.Description:
The Meaicezli Play Purse Sets, available in pink, are being recalled due to a serious risk of injury or death from battery ingestion. These sets include a bag filled with toy accessories such as sunglasses, a camera, money, a makeup kit, lipstick, a wallet, a hair bow, an eye mask, a passport, plastic credit cards, a cell phone, and a writing tablet. Notably, both the toy cell phone and writing tablet are equipped with button or coin cell batteries that can be accessed by young children.

This recall highlights the dangers associated with button cell and coin batteries, which can lead to severe injuries, internal chemical burns, or even fatal outcomes if swallowed. Parents and caregivers are urged to take immediate action to safeguard children from these risks.

recalls.Description:
CreateOn is recalling its Crayola-branded pip-Cubes, which are magnetic building blocks available in Bold Colors and Glitter sets of 24 or 27 cubes. The Bold Colors feature red, green, blue, and yellow cubes, while the Glitter variants come in purple, pink, blue, and green, adorned with glitter. Each cube is marked with the 'Crayola' logo on the left side. The model and UPC numbers can be found on the bottom of the packaging. 

The recalled products pose a serious ingestion hazard as the magnets may detach if the seams fail, potentially leading to severe health risks for children. Swallowed magnets can attract each other or metal objects, resulting in intestinal blockages, perforations, or even fatalities. Consumers are urged to stop using these products immediately and follow the recall instructions.
recalls.Title: CreateOn Recalls Crayola Pip-Cubes Due to Magnet Ingestion Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact CreateOn at 800-333-0549 from 9:30 a.m. to 4 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, via email at pipcuberecall@CreateOn.com, or visit https://www.createon.com/recall/ for more details.

Anzmtosn has issued a recall for its Magnet Fidget Spinner Sets due to serious safety concerns. Each set includes 15 items: four iridescent oval magnets, eight cuboidal magnetic stones, one red and yellow disk fidget spinner, one winged metal spinner, and a multicolored infinity fidget cube, all packaged in a black fabric-lined storage case. These wool-stuffed toys come in various colors such as white, ivory, light brown, dark brown, black, pink, purple, light blue, and mixed shades, with sizes ranging from mini (approximately 6 inches) to large (approximately 19 inches). Innovative Designs has issued a recall for Minecraft Light-Up Slap Ruler Bracelets, specifically Models 718508MCR and 718075MCR, available in pink and green. The product packaging features the "MINECRAFT" logo on the front and the model number on the back. Each bracelet contains three pre-installed button cell batteries, which pose a significant safety risk to children.

The design of the battery compartment allows children to easily access the button cell batteries, leading to potential ingestion. This hazard violates safety standards for battery-operated toys, as swallowing a button cell or coin battery can result in serious injuries, internal chemical burns, or even death. Consumers are urged to take immediate action to ensure the safety of their children.

These ride-on toys are designed in black with red accents and measure 53 inches in length, 43 inches in width, and 27 inches in height. The name 'Torex' is displayed on labels located on both the front and sides of the vehicle. Model numbers 17249 and 17310, along with date codes ranging from 16919 to 11122, can be found beneath the body near the rear wheel. The date codes indicate the year with the last two digits and the day of the year with the first three digits, such as 16919 for June 18, 2019, and 11122 for April 21, 2022.
recalls.Title: Huffy Recalls Torex UTV Ride-On Toys Due to Fire Risk; Available Only at Walmart
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach Huffy Corporation at 800-872-2453 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, via email at torex@huffy.com, or visit www.huffy.com/torex-utv-ride-on-toy-recall. Additional details can be found under 'Customer Service' on the Huffy website by clicking on 'Recall Notices.'

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 20 reports of incidents of the UTVs smoking or catching fire, resulting in five minor burns.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively at Walmart stores nationwide and online at www.walmart.com from August 2019 through December 2024, priced between $500 and $600.

Importers.Name: Huffy Corporation, of Miamisburg, Ohio

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to overheating from improper wiring

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled UTVs and contact Huffy for a free repair. Third-party repairs are allowed, and Huffy will reimburse up to $50 for related costs.

recalls.Description: Leetous has issued a recall for their LED glow sticks and glasses party pack due to a significant risk of injury or death from ingestion. This party pack includes six light-up glasses in various colors (blue, pink, and white) and six glow sticks (orange, green, blue, red, and purple). The items are packaged in a blue box adorned with festive graphics, featuring the Leetous logo on the front and 'Model No. LTXN1003' on the back.
recalls.Title: Leetous Recalls LED Glow Sticks and Glasses Party Pack Due to Serious Ingestion Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, reach out to Leetous at amazonus@leetous.com.

recalls.Description: The Multifunctional Puzzle Crab Toys, which include a magnetic fish hook, eight magnetic pieces, four plastic puzzle pieces, and a plastic mallet, are being recalled due to serious safety concerns. These toys, identified by model number 'HY-99' found on the box's bottom, are packaged in a colorful box labeled 'Multifunctional Puzzle.' The recall is prompted by the toys' violation of federal regulations regarding magnetic toys, which pose a significant ingestion hazard. If swallowed, the powerful magnets can attract each other or other metal objects, potentially leading to severe internal injuries or death.
recalls.Title: Recall of Multifunctional Puzzle Crab Toys Due to Ingestion Risk and Magnet Safety Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled toys, keep them away from children, and reach out to JinJiang Baimei Co. recalls.Description: LaRose Industries is recalling multiple models of Cra-Z-Art Gemex and Gel2Gem jewelry kits due to potential risks of skin, eye, and respiratory irritation. These kits contain a resin that can be harmful when in liquid form and includes an acrylate compound that exceeds safety limits set by the Federal Hazardous Substances Act. Consumers are advised to inspect any jewelry made with these kits to ensure it has fully cured before use. If any liquid or residue is found, the jewelry should not be worn and should be kept away from children.
recalls.Title: LaRose Industries Recalls Cra-Z-Art Gemex and Gel2Gem Jewelry Kits Due to Safety Concerns
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach LaRose Industries at 877-770-7029 from 7:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or via email at gemgel@realtimeresults.net. More information is available at www.recallrtr.com/gemgel or by visiting https://cra-z-art.com/ and clicking on 'Recall' at the bottom of the page.

recalls.Description: The recall affects several models of Auto World Deluxe Pit Kit slot cars, which are sold individually and may be unassembled, painted, or unpainted. Each package features the "Auto World" logo and includes assembly instructions. The recalled models contain two loose magnets that are not secured to the chassis before assembly, posing a serious ingestion hazard for children. Once assembled, the models do not present this risk. The magnets are identified as "Front Magnet" and "Back Magnet" on the parts list on the package, with model numbers and UPCs located on the back as well.
recalls.Title: Recall of Auto World Deluxe Pit Kit Slot Cars Due to Ingestion Risk from Loose Magnets
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Round 2 at 888-770-7120 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email deluxepitkit@realtimeresults.net, or visit http://autoworldstore.com/deluxepitkitrecall for further details.

recalls.Description: The 6-in-1 Pounding Game, featuring a wooden frame with various interactive elements, has been recalled due to a serious safety concern. This game includes a frog clock, a xylophone, colorful gears, and animal-faced pegs that pop up and down. Additionally, it comes with wooden carrots and worms, along with two mallets and a wooden bird that contains a strong magnet, which can detach and pose an ingestion hazard to young children. If swallowed, such magnets can attract one another or other metal objects, potentially causing severe internal injuries.
recalls.Title: Recall of 6-in-1 Pounding Game Due to Ingestion Risk from Loose Magnet; Sold Exclusively on Temu.com
recalls.ConsumerContact: For inquiries, contact DMITOY at dmitoy202405@163.com.

recalls.Description: Pearhead is recalling its Learning Sets, model number 87150, which are marketed as developmental toys for infants aged 0-6 months. Each set contains eight pieces, including a cow stroller toy, a crinkle sheep toy, a black and white tummy time book with flashcards, a plush activity cube, and a wooden egg shaker rattle. The model number can be found on the side of the packaging beneath the barcode.
recalls.Title: Pearhead Recalls Infant Learning Toy Sets Due to Choking Risk and Regulatory Noncompliance
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Pearhead at 888-308-4928, available from 9:30 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email service@pearhead.com or visit https://www.pearhead.com/pages/product-recall for more details.

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to the wooden egg-shaped rattle potentially lodging in a child's throat, violating federal safety regulations for rattles.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to cease use of the egg shaker immediately and contact Pearhead for instructions on returning the product for a free replacement or refund. Pearhead will reach out to all known purchasers directly.

recalls.Description: Elongdi has issued a recall for their multicolored magnetic building sticks sets, which contain 130 pieces, including 100 magnetic sticks and 30 steel balls. These sets are packaged in a box featuring the "Elongdi" logo and the phrase "Magnetic Sticks" on both sides. The item number associated with this product is E107. The recall is due to a violation of federal safety regulations concerning toy magnets, which pose a serious ingestion risk.
recalls.Title: Elongdi Magnetic Building Sticks Sets Recalled for Ingestion Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Elongdi at 202-375-0895 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or via email at support@elongdi.net.

Images.Caption: Recalled Magnetic Building Sticks Set, 130 Pieces with 100 Magnetic Sticks
Packaging of the Recalled Magnetic Sticks Set (Front of Box)
Packaging of the Recalled Magnetic Sticks Set (Back of Box)

Injuries.Name: None reported. CPSC estimates 2,400 magnet ingestions were treated in hospital emergency departments from 2017 through 2021. CPSC is aware of eight deaths since 2005 through 2021 involving the ingestion of hazardous magnets, including two outside of the United States.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at Amazon.com from March 2024 to June 2024 for approximately $20.

Distributors.Name: Elongdi, of China

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to high-powered magnets

Remedies.Name: Stop using the product and return it for a full refund

recalls.metaDescription: Elongdi recalls Magnetic Building Sticks Sets due to ingestion hazards. Contact them for a refund and stop using the product immediately.

The ornament, which stands approximately 4.5 inches tall and 1.5 inches wide, depicts the character wearing a brown hat and jacket, with a green face and blue cuffs. It was packaged in a collectible white storage box with red accents, marked with the code 2199QXE3341 on the bottom.
recalls.Title: Hallmark Recalls Cad Bane Christmas Tree Ornaments Due to Mold Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Hallmark at 800-425-5627 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. You can also email them via the 'Contact Us' section at https://care.hallmark.com/s/ or visit www.hallmark.com/product-recalls for further details.

They are equipped with music playback and a detachable hook for easy attachment to car seats and strollers. The Dreamgro name and model numbers can be found on the product tag. The affected models include the Light Blue Mermaid (Model DF156-MT) and Pink Mermaid (Model DF156-LI).
recalls.Title: Dreamgro Recalls Lullaby Travel Soothers Due to Choking Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the affected soothers immediately and keep them away from children. For assistance, contact Dreamgro at 888-202-0442 from 9 a.m. to 3 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email Recall@dreamgro.com, or visit www.dreamgro.com/recall.html. It is identifiable by a hang tag that reads 'wee gallery,' 'tummy time gallery,' and 'wildlife' along with additional text.

recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Wee Gallery at 800-282-5149 between 9 a.m. and 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. You can also email tummytimerecall@weegallery.com or visit weegallery.com/tummytimerecall for more details.

recalls.Description: Intellio Toys is recalling its Bright Builder fort kits, including models FKL-BG, FKL-BR, L-5BG, and L-5BR. The kits are marked with "Bright Builder" on the front and "Intellio Toys" on the side, with the model number located on the bottom right corner of the back of the box. This recall specifically affects kits that include flashlights.
recalls.Title: Intellio Toys Recalls Bright Builder Fort Kits Due to Battery Ingestion Risk

The set includes a wheelbarrow, apron, gloves, and various hand tools, with the wheelbarrow marked with a white label indicating the manufacturing date of December 2023.
recalls.Title: Red Toolbox Issues Recall for Stanley Jr. Kids Garden Sets Due to Lead Poisoning Risk

recalls.Description:
At Home has issued a recall for Tie Dye Fur Toy Trunk Storage Chests, available in two sizes. The large chest measures approximately 32-½" x 16-½" x 21", while the small version is about 27" x 13-½" x 16". Constructed from MDF wood and covered with a vibrant tie dye faux fur fabric, these chests pose serious safety concerns. Without a lid support feature or ventilation holes, they present risks of entrapment and suffocation to children.

Consumers are urged to stop using these toy storage chests immediately. To receive a full refund, return the product to any At Home location. The chests were sold in stores across the U.S. and online at athome.com between September 2023 and February 2024, with prices ranging from $50 to $60.

recalls.Description:
MGA Entertainment has announced a recall of its Miniverse Make It Mini Sets that include unused liquid resins. The affected products feature various themes such as 'Make It Mini Appliances,' 'Make It Mini Food,' and 'Make It Mini Lifestyle.' Each set contains a sphere with materials to create miniature versions of food and appliances, with resin packaging that can resemble food items. These sets may also be sold in bundles or dedicated boxes, all marked with specific product information and UPC codes on the packaging.

The recalled sets may pose health risks, as the liquid resins can cause irritation or sensitization to the skin, eyes, and respiratory system. This hazard arises from the presence of acrylates in the resins, which are restricted in children's products under federal law. Once the resins cure, they no longer present these risks.
recalls.Title: MGA Entertainment Issues Recall for Miniverse Make It Mini Sets Due to Potential Skin, Eye, and Respiratory Irritation
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact MGA Entertainment at 800-222-4685 from 9 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, or 9 a.m. to 2 p.m. ET Saturday. They can also reach out via email at mvcustomer_care@mgae.com or visit https://www.mgae.com/customer-care/recalls for more details.

recalls.Description:
Thousandshores has issued a recall for Theefun Kids Gardening Tools sets, model number HJL-001. These sets include five garden tools with wooden handles, each featuring a brown handle string, and measure approximately 27 inches long. The recall is due to the presence of phthalates in the handle strings, which exceed federal safety limits and pose health risks to young children if ingested.

Consumers are urged to remove these gardening tools from children immediately. Thousandshores is offering a full refund and advises consumers to cut off and safely dispose of the brown handle strings according to local recycling guidelines. The company, along with Amazon, is actively reaching out to all known purchasers to ensure safety compliance.
recalls.Title: Thousandshores Recalls Theefun Kids Gardening Tools Due to Phthalate Concerns
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Thousandshores via email at support@theefun.com or visit their customer service page at https://www.amazon.com/sp?ie=UTF8&seller=A3G27BXXOHYJZ1.

Hazards.Name: Excessive phthalate levels in handle strings pose ingestion risks to young children.

recalls.Description: Magnetic chess games containing 20 small, spherical magnet stones have been recalled due to safety concerns. These games are packaged in a blue box labeled with terms like "Magnetic," "Magnetism," or "Magnetism Intelligence Strategy Game." Each set includes approximately 20 loose magnets, a string, a plastic storage tray with sponge lining, and gameplay instructions. The strong magnetic flux of these pieces poses a serious ingestion hazard.
recalls.Title: Recall of Magnetic Chess Games Due to Ingestion Risk; Violates Federal Toy Magnet Regulations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out to Outad Good Life via email at pdhnfgjg@163.com, or send a message through the "message" icon on Temu's seller profile. You can also check "Your Recalls and Product Safety Alerts" on Temu at www.temu.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled 1 Set Puzzle Magnetic Battle Chess with 20 Magnetic Pieces
Packaging of the recalled 1 Set Puzzle Magnetic Battle Chess Game (Front of Box)
Recalled 1 Set Puzzle Magnetic Battle Chess Game contents

Injuries.Name: None reported. CPSC estimates 2,400 magnet ingestions were treated in hospital emergency departments from 2017 through 2021. CPSC is aware of seven deaths since 2005 involving the ingestion of hazardous magnets, including two outside of the United States.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at Temu.com from December 2023 to February 2024 for approximately $8.

Distributors.Name: Outad Good Life Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: The magnetic chess games violate federal toy magnet regulations due to the presence of small magnets that can be ingested. If swallowed, these powerful magnets can attract each other or other metal objects, leading to severe health risks such as intestinal perforations, blockages, infections, and even death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should immediately cease using the recalled magnetic chess games and keep them away from children. Contact Outad Good Life for a full refund. Alternatively, visit your Temu account, locate the order in "Your Orders," and click the "Return/Refund" button. Follow the instructions on the Return and Refund Policy page on Temu.com to obtain a free return shipping label. Outad Good Life will also notify all known purchasers directly.

recalls.Description: DailySale is recalling its 216-piece set of 5mm Magnetic DIY Balls Magic Cubes due to significant ingestion hazards. These small, spherical neodymium magnets are known for their strong magnetic properties and were sold in colorful sets. The recalled products were packaged in a clear plastic case and a tin storage box, posing a risk to children and others if ingested.
recalls.Title: DailySale Recalls 5mm Magnetic DIY Balls Due to Serious Ingestion Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach DailySale via email at recall@dailysale.com or visit https://dailysale.com/pages/recall and select "Recall Notice".

Images.Caption: Recalled 216 Piece 5mm Magnetic DIY Balls Magic Cube
Recalled 216 Piece 5mm Magnetic DIY Balls Magic Cube (enclosed in plastic case)

Injuries.Name: None reported. CPSC estimates 2,400 magnet ingestions were treated in hospital emergency departments from 2017 through 2021. CPSC is aware of seven deaths since 2005 involving the ingestion of hazardous magnets, including two outside of the United States.

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.dailysale.com from July 2021 to May 2022 for approximately $30.

Importers.Name: DailySale Inc., of Pompano Beach, Florida

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to strong magnets that can cause serious internal injuries.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the product immediately, keep it away from children, and contact DailySale for a prepaid return label to receive a full refund.

recalls.metaDescription: DailySale recalls 5mm Magnetic DIY Balls due to ingestion hazards. Contact them for refunds and safety information.

recalls.Description: Huihuang Trading Inc. is recalling its Fishing Games due to a serious hazard involving magnets that can be ingested. The game includes nine animal pieces, four toy fishing rods, four toy fishing nets, four markers, game instructions, and a waterproof storage bag. The batch number X002903XZT323 can be found on the side of the game's packaging. This recall affects products sold exclusively on Amazon.com between June and July 2023.
recalls.Title: Huihuang Trading Recalls Fishing Games Due to Magnet Ingestion Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact Huihuang Trading Inc. via email at safety@operationagency.com for assistance.

recalls.Description: The Yaomiao children's rhinestone silver tiara, featuring red rhinestones, is being recalled due to lead levels that exceed federal safety standards. This product was sold in a set that included three other tiara styles—blue, purple, and clear rhinestones—that are not part of the recall. All four tiaras were packaged together under the brand name 'Yaomiao'.
recalls.Title: Recall of Yaomiao Children's Rhinestone Silver Tiaras Due to Lead Content Violation
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further details, reach out to LordRoadS via email at simlordroadple@163.com, or visit their Amazon storefront at https://www.amazon.com/s?me=A1ISSCMO2YO2PL&marketplaceID=ATVPDKIKX0DER.

Images.Caption: Recalled Yaomiao Red Rhinestone Silver Tiara

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from January 2021 to November 2023, priced around $19 for the set.

Distributors.Name: Wuhannuoyunxindianzikejiyouxiangongsi (dba LordRoadS), of China

Hazards.Name: Lead content exceeding federal safety limits poses health risks if ingested by children.

Remedies.Name: Immediately remove the tiaras from children, cease usage, and contact LordRoadS for a refund. LordRoadS and Amazon are reaching out to all known buyers.

recalls.Description: A recall has been issued for multiple dress-up playsets from Litti City and Litti Pritti, as well as Prextex slime eggs. The Litti City premier doctor playset features a white doctor outfit with accessories like blue glasses and a yellow stethoscope. The teacher purse playset includes a green purse with various educational tools. The Litti Pritti handbag playset comes with a pink purse and makeup items. The Prextex slime eggs, packaged in a blue container, contain colorful slime and mini bottles of shaped beads. These products have been found to exceed federal safety standards for phthalates and lead content, posing health risks to children.
recalls.Title: Recall of Litti City and Litti Pritti Dress-Up Playsets and Prextex Slime Eggs Due to Phthalates and Lead Violations

recalls.Description: Primark is recalling its baby rattles, specifically the "Wooden Music Shaker" and "DTR M&M Maracas" due to safety concerns. These rattles were available in vibrant colors such as yellow, red, light green, and light blue. Notably, the red rattle features a "Minnie Mouse" design, while the yellow one showcases a "Mickey Mouse" image. The product packaging includes the numbers 7495201 for the Wooden Music Shaker and 5311101 for the DTR M&M Maracas.
recalls.Title: Primark Recalls Baby Rattles Due to Choking and Ingestion Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can contact Primark U.S. at 617-946-3236 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit https://www.primark.com/en-us/help/recall-wooden-shaker. Alternatively, visit www.primark.com and click on "Recall" at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Primark "DTR M&M Maracas" baby rattles
Recalled Primark "Wooden Music Shaker" baby rattles

Injuries.Name: The firm has received five reports of the rattles breaking apart in the United Kingdom. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Primark U.S. locations in the northeast, Florida, Maryland, and Chicago, available from March 2021 to August 2023, priced between $3.50 and $4.50

Importers.Name: Primark U.S. Corp., of Boston

Hazards.Name: Risk of choking and ingestion due to detachable parts

Remedies.Name: Return the rattles to any Primark store for a full refund

recalls.Description:
XpressGoods is recalling its 5mm Neodymium Magnetic Balls, which are small, spherical magnets known for their strong magnetic properties. These colorful magnetic balls were sold in sets and were packaged in a clear plastic case or a tin storage box. The recall is due to safety concerns regarding the potential ingestion of these powerful magnets by children.

The Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) has determined that these magnetic ball sets do not meet safety regulations, as they contain magnets that can fit into small parts and possess excessive strength. If swallowed, these magnets can attract each other or other metal objects, leading to severe health risks including intestinal blockage, infection, and even death.
recalls.Title: XpressGoods Recalls 5mm Neodymium Magnetic Balls Due to Serious Ingestion Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact XpressGoods via their website at xpressgoods.com, email support@xpressgoods.com, or visit https://xpressgoods.com/product-recalls/ and click on 'Recall Notice'.

Images.Caption: Recalled Colorful Metal Neodymium Magic Magnetic Balls - 8 color, 5mm

Injuries.Name: None reported. CPSC estimates 2,400 magnet ingestions were treated in hospital emergency departments from 2017 through 2021. CPSC is aware of seven deaths involving the ingestion of hazardous magnets, including two outside of the United States.

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.xpressgoods.com from July 2021 through May 2022 for approximately $30.

Importers.Name: Xpressgoods.com, of Raleigh, North Carolina

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to strong magnets that can cause serious internal injuries

Remedies.Name: Stop using the product immediately, keep it away from children, and contact XpressGoods for a prepaid return label for a full refund or store credit.

recalls.metaDescription: XpressGoods recalls 5mm Neodymium Magnetic Balls due to ingestion hazards. Stop using immediately and contact for a refund.

recalls.Description: Mary Meyer is recalling specific Bubba Bull Plush Toys (Style 45541) with the date code 1945, which is located on a white tag attached to the toy. These plush toys feature a brown body with off-white bull horns and have the Mary Meyer label on the seam. The toys may pose a choking hazard as the eyes can detach, presenting a risk to young children.
recalls.Title: Mary Meyer Recalls Bubba Bull Plush Toys Due to Choking Hazard Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach out to Mary Meyer Corp. at 800-451-4387, Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET. They can also email Recall@marymeyer.com or visit www.marymeyer.com/recall/ for more details.
